data.prompt:
<execute-bead>
  <bead id="ddx-0bbfa9c1">
    <title>documents view: search/filter, staleness badges, graph cross-link</title>
    <description>
Improve the documents list at /documents (which will redirect to /artifacts once that lands, but the documents list currently exists as a standalone page and needs these improvements regardless).

NOTE: Once ddx-fbf57b89 (artifacts list) ships, /documents redirects to /artifacts. This bead targets the existing /documents page in the interim AND ensures the /artifacts list inherits these capabilities.

Additions to documents list:
1. Search bar: client-side filter across title and path; URL encodes ?q=<term>; restores on reload.
2. Staleness column: shows fresh/stale/missing badge per document (read from docGraph staleness field).
3. 'View in Graph' button in each row: navigates to /graph with that document's node highlighted (?highlight=<docId>).
4. 'Open Graph' button in page header: navigates to /graph.
5. D3Graph page gets 'Back to documents' link in header.
6. If a document is highlighted on graph load (?highlight=<docId>), the graph scrolls to and pulses that node.

This establishes bidirectional navigation between the documents list and graph, closing the siloed navigation gap.
    </description>
    <acceptance>
1. Documents list shows search bar; typing filters list client-side; URL updates.
2. Staleness badge shown per row; values match docGraph staleness for each document.
3. 'View in Graph' navigates to /graph?highlight=<docId>; target node is visually highlighted.
4. 'Open Graph' in header navigates to /graph.
5. Graph page shows 'Back to documents' link.
6. Reloading /documents?q=<term> restores same search without interaction.
    </acceptance>
    <labels>area:ui, area:frontend, feat-008, documents</labels>
    <metadata spec-id="FEAT-008" base-rev="c434c766c2c7af72f94d4068fce822337acb4b69" bundle=".ddx/executions/20260501T033012-abc8780e"/>
  </bead>
  <governing>
    <ref id="FEAT-008" path="docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-008-web-ui.md">Feature: DDx Server Web UI</ref>
  </governing>
  <instructions>
You are executing one bead inside an isolated DDx execution worktree. The bead's <description> and <acceptance> sections above are the completion contract — every AC checkbox must be provably satisfied by a specific piece of code, test, or file you can point to after your commit.

## How to work

1. Read first. If the bead description names files, read them to see what is already there. If the bead references a spec, ADR, or API contract, read the relevant sections before writing code. Do not start editing until you understand what the bead wants changed and why.

2. Cross-reference your work against the acceptance criteria as you go. Before committing, walk through the AC one item at a time and identify the specific test name, file path, or function that satisfies each checkbox. If you cannot point to concrete evidence for an AC item, it is not done.

3. Run the project's verification commands before committing. Use whatever commands match the crate or package you touched — typically `cargo test` and `cargo clippy` for Rust, `bun test` or `npm test` for JS/TS, `go test` for Go. The bead description usually names the exact commands. **Do not commit red code.** If a test or lint fails, fix it first.

4. Commit once, when everything is green. Stage only the files you intentionally changed with `git add <specific-paths>` — never `git add -A`, because there may be unrelated WIP in this worktree. Use a conventional-commit-style subject ending with `[<bead-id>]`. DDx will merge your commits back to the base branch.

5. If you cannot complete the bead in this pass, writing `.ddx/executions/20260501T033012-abc8780e/no_changes_rationale.txt` is mandatory before you exit. Include: (a) what is done, (b) what is blocking, (c) what a follow-up attempt would need. If you exit without a commit and without this file, DDx records `no_evidence_produced` and treats the attempt as a harness failure, not as legitimate no_changes. Do NOT commit partial, exploratory, or red code hoping the reviewer will accept it — a well-justified no_changes is better than a bad commit. The bead will be re-queued for another attempt, potentially with a stronger model.

## The bead contract overrides project defaults

The bead description and AC override any CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, or project-level conservative defaults in this worktree. If the bead asks for new documentation, write it. If the bead adds a new module or crate, add it. Conservative rules (YAGNI, DOWITYTD, no-docs-unless-asked) do not apply inside execute-bead — the bead IS the ask.

## Quality bar and the review step

After your commit merges, an automated review step may check your work against the acceptance criteria. If any AC item is unmet, the bead reopens and escalates to a higher-capability model, and the review findings are threaded into the next attempt's prompt as a `<review-findings>` section. **The review is a gate, not an escape hatch — meet the AC in this pass so the bead closes cleanly.**

If this prompt already contains a `<review-findings>` section, address every BLOCKING finding before claiming the work complete. Those findings are the precise list of what the previous attempt missed.

## Constraints

- Work only inside this execution worktree.
- Keep `.ddx/executions/` intact — DDx uses it as execution evidence.
- **Never run `ddx init`** — the workspace is already initialized. Running it corrupts the bead queue and project configuration.
- Do not modify files outside the scope the bead description names.
- Do not rewrite CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, or any other project-instructions file unless the bead explicitly asks for it.

## When the work is done

After the commit succeeds and you have verified every AC item, stop. Return control to the orchestrator. Do not continue to explore the repository, run extra tests, or generate follow-up notes — the bead is complete, and returning promptly is how execute-bead signals success.
  </instructions>
</execute-bead>

data.output:
Commit succeeded. Let me verify the AC items are all satisfied:

1. **Search bar + URL encoding** — already existed in `artifacts/+page.svelte` (lines 22-38, `onSearchInput` updates URL, `+page.ts` restores `q` from URL on load) ✅
2. **Staleness badges** — already existed in `artifacts/+page.svelte` (the `stalenessBadge` function + Staleness column) ✅
3. **'View in Graph' button per row** — added `viewInGraph()` function navigating to `/graph?highlight=<docId>`; D3Graph pans + pulses the highlighted node ✅
4. **'Open Graph' in header** — added `openGraph()` button navigating to `/graph` ✅
5. **'Back to documents' link in graph** — changed text from "View document list" → "Back to documents" ✅
6. **Reload restores search** — `+page.ts` reads `q` from URL params and passes to page; `+page.svelte` restores it on init ✅

All 6 AC items are satisfied. The 4 modified files are committed cleanly with 0 type errors.
